commands.command: — addressing the codex review for `70700cf707` (now `cb30b0d382`). ## p2: encode non-ascii filenames in content-disposition — fixed > "node's `serverresponse.setheader` throws `err_invalid_char` for names like > `価格.xlsx`, and because this call is inside the route `try`, those downloads > become a 404. use an ascii fallback plus rfc 5987 `filename*`, and cover it > with a real header-validation regression." done in `cb30b0d382`. new helper `builddownloadcontentdispositionheader()` emits rfc 5987 / 6266 §3.2.4-compliant headers: - **pure ascii** (e.g. `pricing.xlsx`) → `attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx"` (legacy single-token form preserved; no churn on the common case) - **non-ascii** (e.g. `価格.xlsx`, `résumé.docx`) → `attachment; filename="<ascii-fallback>"; filename*=utf-8''<percent-encoded>` (latin-1-safe fallback for legacy clients, utf-8-precise `filename*` for modern browsers and curl) ```diff +function builddownloadcontentdispositionheader(safefilename: string): string { + const islatin1safe = /^[\x20-\x7e\xa0-\xff]*$/u.test(safefilename); + const asciifallback = safefilename.replace(/[^\x20-\x7e]/gu, "_"); + const utf8encoded = encodeuricomponent(safefilename) + .replace(/['()]/g, (c) => "%" + c.charcodeat(0).tostring(16).touppercase()) + .replace(/\*/g, "%2a"); + if (islatin1safe && asciifallback === safefilename) { + return `attachment; filename="${safefilename}"`; + } + return `attachment; filename="${asciifallback}"; filename*=utf-8''${utf8encoded}`; +} ``` ### real header-validation regression coverage — added three new tests in `control-ui.http.test.ts` use a real `http.createserver()` (not `makemockhttpresponse`, which codex correctly noted does not enforce node's runtime header character validation): ``` ✓ serves a cjk filename without err_invalid_char (uses rfc 5987 filename*) ✓ serves a latin-1 accented filename via the ascii fallback path ✓ keeps the legacy single-token form for pure-ascii filenames ``` `pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/control-ui.http.test.ts`: **165/165 passing** (was 156; +9 = 3 tests × 3 vitest projects). ## p3: changelog entry — added ``` ### fixes - gateway/chat: serve assistant-media binary downloads (xlsx/csv/pdf/zip/etc) with `content-disposition: attachment` and an rfc 5987 / 6266 `filename*=utf-8''…` value plus an ascii fallback so non-image documents trigger a real download instead of letting the browser render them as a webpage and offer save as "webpage complete". document attachment anchors in the chat surface also receive a `download` attribute and the managed `/api/chat/media/outgoing-doc/` url is no longer double-wrapped through `/__openclaw__/assistant-media`. (#77912) ``` ## real behavior proof — before/after with a real http.server drives the patched handler against three real fixtures via `http.createserver()` + `http.request()` to 127.0.0.1. ### after fix (`cb30b0d382`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ascii — control) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="pricing.xlsx" ✓ pass === résumé.docx (latin-1 accented) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document content-disposition: attachment; filename="r_sum_.docx"; filename*=utf-8''r%c3%a9sum%c3%a9.docx ✓ pass === 価格.xlsx (cjk — was throwing err_invalid_char before this fix) === http 200 content-type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet content-disposition: attachment; filename="__.xlsx"; filename*=utf-8''%e4%be%a1%e6%a0%bc.xlsx ✓ pass 3 passed, 0 failed ``` ### before fix (same harness, fix reverted via `git stash`) ``` === pricing.xlsx (pure ascii — control) === http 200 content-disposition: attachment;
